steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The skill is rhetorical synthesis. To emphasize how long Edgson competed, the answer must explicitly state both a start year and an end year, giving the reader the full span of his career.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘During his career as a Paralympic swimmer, Michael Edgson took home a total of twenty-one medals, eighteen of which were gold.’: Focuses on medal count, not duration — no years are mentioned, so the length of his career is never stated. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘An accomplished Paralympian with multiple gold medal wins, Michael Edgson competed as a swimmer from 1984 to 1992.’: Gives both the start year (1984) and end year (1992), explicitly showing the span of his competition — the most direct way to emphasize duration. ✓
  • Choice C — ‘Swimming, a sport in which Michael Edgson took home eighteen gold medals, has been an event at the Paralympic Games since 1960.’: Mentions the sport's history, not Edgson's competition years; his career duration is absent. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘Cross-country skier Terje Loevaas made his Paralympic debut in 1980, while swimmer Michael Edgson made his debut in 1984.’: States only Edgson's debut year (1984), omitting when he finished competing — a single year cannot convey duration. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
